147 - Sales Objection Handling - Sales Training Technique with Jeremy Miner - YouTube.mp4
Now in this video, you're gonna learn what to say and more importantly what to ask when your potential customer throws out the question, 'Why should I go with you?' Now, you hear this as a salesperson frequently. When your prospect says, 'John or Mary?' 'Why should I go with you or... Why should we go with your company?' What's the typical response to this question by the average salesperson? Ask yourself. How do you respond? Now, most salespeople that come to me for training, it usually goes something like this. Well, I mean, Mr. Smith, I mean, why wouldn't you go with this? I mean, we've been rated the number one dealer. We've got the best price on this car. I mean, we actually have the way better customer service than XYZ company does.
And I tell you what, this new air conditioning system in this car, completely better than the cars over at XYZ. So what is a salesperson doing? He's just listing the benefits and features of his or her solution. But the problem with this approach of telling and trying to persuade your potential customer to go with you is what? Well, once again, it's entirely focused on you and your own agenda of making the sale rather than focused on your potential customers and seeing if you can help them. So it's you trying to persuade them instead of asking skilled questions that allow them to persuade themselves. Which is more persuasive? You trying to persuade them or them persuading themselves that they should buy from you? Now, we already know the answer.
And we already know from behavioral science that telling someone what they should do, presenting your story, and trying to persuade them is the least effective form of selling and persuasion. So the question is, Why do you keep doing it thinking it's actually going to work? And what happens when you keep doing it? What usually happens 80, 90% of the time? Well, as you know, your prospects throw out objections and they go with someone else. So instead of trying to tell them why they should go with you, why not find out more about why they even asked you the question in the first place or what's behind this question? Why are they asking you? Here's what the seven-figure person would ask. Mr. Smith says, 'Mary, why should I go with you?' Mary would say, 'Well, Mr.
Smith, I'm not... quite convinced yet that you should.' You're then going to pause for three seconds. The seven-figure salesperson says this with confidence, yet in a calm manner. When your prospect sees you're detached from making the sale, they will feel like you really care and you're really there to help them, not just trying to sell them something. So instead of you looking like just another salesperson in their minds trying to sell them something and stuff something down their throat, they now start viewing you as a trusted authority. They view you as an advisor that's there to help them. And the best part of this is they'll always tell you the truth. Do you know why? It's because they trust you. Remember, Mr. Smith has just asked you.
Tell me, Mary, why should I go with you? Here's what the seven-figure person is going to ask, and then we'll transition into the next statement. Well, Mr. Smith, I'm not quite convinced that you should yet. And it might be appropriate if we asked each other a few questions to find out a little bit more about you and what you're possibly looking for to see if we could even help you. Now, you will start to ask a few background questions after this to find out what their history is and to find out if they have problems and they're prepared to change their situation. And let's say that for this example, you're selling cars. We'll give an example of financial services, maybe insurance products. Like I said, it works for anything.
For example, what type of car do you drive now? would ask that if you're selling cars or, for example, what type of investments are you currently in. If you're selling financial services or... What do you do for investing? Or let's say you sell insurance. For example, what type of insurance do you have now? You see, the seven-figure approach, the new model of selling, is used with anything that you sell. It doesn't matter if it's pens, to toilet bowls, to cars, to real estate, to investing, to selling jumbo jets or recruiting. It's all the same principles. It's the new model. I will tell you. This is a difference between you making $50,000 a year, $100,000 a year, $250,000 a year, half a million dollars a year, and over a million dollars a year in your sales career.
These little things add up and will separate you from your competition and put you in the top 1% in your company and your entire industry. Now, you can go from just getting average results to becoming a high six-figure and even seven-figure earner if that's what you want. Now, I want to thank you for watching this video about what to say and more importantly, what to ask when your potential customer throws out the question, 'Why should I go with you?' Now, in the next video, we're going to discuss how your enthusiasm, your excitement about what you're selling— product or service— is actually causing you to lose sales and how you can change that.
